DAVID B. PARUELEE
Attorney At Law
1624 TYIer Street
Berkel-ey, Ca1if orn j-a 94703
(415) 64 4 -154 8
Decenber 5, 19 90
Ms. Karen Haney-o$/ens, Associate Planner
Planning Departtnent
THE CITY OF BERKELEY
2180 l.lilvia Street, 2nd Floor
Berkeley, CA 947 04
Dear l.Is . Haney-owens :
Pfease let this letter 
confirm our conversation on
Thursday, November 29, 1990, wherein you agreed to set up a
Eeeting in the very near future to discuss the down-zoning in
District Three (3). 
As you knolr, lre have been attenpting in
good faith since February, 1990 to have this issue decided. The
Planning Commission's latest maneuver is perceived by nany to be
the rtlast strawtr in a pattern of bad faith actions on behalf of
the city.
we agreed that a neeting among interested and concerned
parties may function to stave-off any intended litigation. 
To
that end, we request that representatives of the mayorrs office,
city 
managerrs office 
and city 
attorneyts offj.ce be in
attendance. we would also expect the attendance of an approved
Planning Commission nember. commissioner Patrick Devaney is
quite familiar wi.th_this issue and we erould be pleased with his
input. 
our group will be represented by Deborah caldon and
nyself.
once rre have set a date, we will 
also seek
representatives' attendance from other neighborhood groups in
the impacted area. we will expect to hear from you in the near
future with possible dates for this meeting.


Ms. Karen Haney-Or{ens
December 5, 199 O
Page 2
You informed me that it woutd be politically 
expedient to
request that Planning Director ciL KelLy attend this neeting.
We are formally stating that we have no interest in Mr. Kellyrs
input. 
We inform you that a major basis of any lalrsuit against
the City over this issue is Mr. Kelly's perceived and continued
lack of good faith in dealing with our group. I{e have come to
understand that it was at Mr. Kellyts directive that none of our
correspondence of Spring, 1990 lras responded to; we believe this
actionable in i-tself 
and at the very least 
extremely
inappropriate for a professional in his position. 
We certainly
do not approve of his behavior in any manner during the course
of our dealing with the planning department.
We find further evidence of Mr. Kellyrs lack of good
faith with regard to our group in that he was on the phone to
Patrick Devaney approxinately tuo to three hours after 
my
conversation with you. 
It 
appears the purpose of this
conversation was to try and rrcut a dealrr on this vital
neighborhood issue. we believe that this smacks of the above-
referenced bad faith. 
we do not believe that L/e are being
unreasonable in our request that l,{r. Ke11y no longer be involved
in this process.
We strongly suggest before the neeting that you create,
on the Cityrs behalf, a chronological tiure line of the Cityrs
actions (inactions ?) regarding our concerns since we first
raised the issue in February, 1990. Perhaps it will put your
reLiance on non-conpliance with Robertrs Rules of orders in the
proper perspective with regard to the Cityrs lack of good faith.
Under the Freedom of Information Act, we are fornal.ly
requesting that you inrnediately forward to us any and all
docurnents, notes, memoranda, etc. concerninq the investigation
of the Planning Commissionrs original vote. 
We specifj.cally
request the narne or nanes of those individuals on the Planning
cornmission or not who requested the city Attorney investigate
the vote.
Further, on December 4, L99o, I received your Notice of
Special Planning Commission Meeting. This is to inforrn you that
our group considers this to be insufficient 
notice, under
' California 1aw, regarding the tour referenced on Friday,
December 7, l-990. We agaj.n are surprised by your actions in
rrnotifying" the public that they are invited 1o a tour that
begins at 10:OO a.m. on a business day when almost every
concerned citizen is working.


Ms. Karen Eaney-Owens
December 5, l-99O
Page 3
we formally request that you reschedule this toirr to a
weekend in the near future so that the Planning Commission can
receive active citizen input. This, of course, by necessity,
and with proper notice, will cause the action to reclassify sub-
area three (3), schedul,ed for the next Planning commission
neeting on December ]-2, l99o to be rescheduled.
Ouite frankly, our gEoup has talked to many citizens j"n
the impacted area and there is a sense of bewilderment and anger
at what is perceived to be another unilaterally 
political
decision by the cj.ty of Berkeley and its Planning Commission to
do something that clearLy contravenes the wishes of the rnajority
of the affected citj-zens. we hope the neeting, hereby forrnally
requested, will hetp to resolve this dispute in an amicable
manner and a manner in which neighborhood input is for once
truly considered.
we also requested of your office that a list of every
decision by the Planning cornrnission in which lilax Anderson
participated in since his reappointment, be forwarded to the
above address. As of this date, that information has not been
forthconing. It is our position that l.{r. Anderson, especially
since having been found to have a conflict of interest by the
city Attorney, (and still 
under investigation by the FPPC) is
unfit to hold office on the Planning Commission. we have
reviewed the Berkeley Commissionerrs lIanual and find 
no
guidance .
By copy of this letter to the City Attorney, we are
fornally requesting that this guestion be investigated. Please
be assured that $/e witl- be conducting our own investigation of
the proper laws in this regard. 
We believe it is important
enough to initiate the filinq of another cornplaint with the FPPC
if the city Attorney or if the city of Berkeley is not
forthconing with a decision that has an actual basis in law in
the near future.
Hoqrever, we belj,eve the j-ssue is important enough that
the city Attorney should direct Mr. Anderson to no longer
participate in any Planning Commission activities 
until his
position 
on that 
Comnission is 
deemed Iegitimate 
and
appropriate. We are also investigating his participation in the
Lorin Station Project for violations of city and state laws.


l,ls . Karen Haney-owens
December 5, 199 O
Paqe 4
Finally, please be assured that this letter is written
based on inforroation and belief in a good faith atternpt to try
to resolve these issues with the city. 
we will expect that the
city will treat our concerns in a like manner and respond to
then in the very near and iuunediate future. Thank you for your
attention to these matters.
Very truly yours,
Manuela Albuquerque, city Attorney
city of Berkeley
Menbers of the Board of Planning Comrnission
City of Berkeley

B. PARMELEE
DBP/ 1ny

City of Berkeley

sections.question_synthetic: This is not an FPPC advice letter but rather correspondence from an attorney to a city planner regarding zoning disputes, planning commission procedures, and allegations of conflict of interest involving a planning commissioner.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This document is incoming correspondence (not an FPPC response letter). It references an FPPC investigation of Max Anderson but is not itself an FPPC advice letter. No question/conclusion sections present. Document mentions threat to file FPPC complaint but this is correspondence from a private attorney to city officials regarding local zoning/planning disputes.

embedding.summary: This is a December 5, 1990 letter from attorney David B. Parmelee to Berkeley Planning Department staff regarding ongoing disputes over District Three down-zoning, requesting a meeting with city officials and raising concerns about Planning Director Gil Kelly's conduct, insufficient public notice for a planning commission tour, and conflicts of interest involving Planning Commissioner Max Anderson who is under FPPC investigation.
